chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Special Sales Tax Reference Application 328 of 2018 ___________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) ___________________________________________________________ 1. For orders on office objection No.18 2. For orders on CMA No.2736/2018 3. For hearing of main case 09.10.2025 This matter is pending since 2018 without any progress. On 22.01.2021, following order was passed: “None for the applicant, no intimation is received. In the interest of justice, adjourned for a date to be fixed after four weeks, however, with the caution that if nobody appears on behalf the applicant no the next date, instant reference application may be dismissed for non-prosecution. Let intimation notice be issued to the counsel for the applicant for the next date” On 25.09.2025 following order was passed: “Mr. Muhammad Bilal Bhatti, advocate for the applicant Matter is pending since 2018 without any progress. Counsel seeks time to reformulate the questions of law. At his request adjourned to come up after one week” Today applicant remains unrepresented without intimation or justification. In view of judgment of the Supreme Court in the case of C.I.R. vs. Rafeh Limited reported as PLD 2020 SC 518, this reference application is dismissed for non-prosecution. A copy of this order may be sent under the seal of this Court and the signature of the Registrar to the learned Appellate Tribunal, as required per section 47 subsection 5 of Sales Tax Act, 1990. Judge Judge Amjad
